design_philosophy.name: The Control Tower
design_philosophy.description: A high-density, low-latency visual system designed for clarity in chaos. It rejects the 'soft' aesthetics of modern SaaS (rounded corners, excessive padding, gradients) in favor of 'Swiss Industrial' precision. Every pixel is data-bearing. Beauty comes from alignment and legibility, not decoration.
core_principles: Density is Information: Don't hide complexity, organize it.
The 'Paper Trail' Metaphor: Use borders and distinct surfaces rather than floating shadows.
Monospace is King: All IDs, dates, and metrics must use monospace fonts for rapid scanning.
Safety Colors: Use standard industrial safety colors (Signal Red, Warning Yellow, Safe Green) for status, not pastel variations.

layout.structure: Sidebar (Left, Fixed, 64px/240px) + Header (Top, Fixed, h-14) + Main Content (Scrollable, p-6)

buttons.primary: bg-slate-900 text-white hover:bg-slate-800 rounded-sm px-4 py-2 font-medium transition-colors shadow-sm
buttons.secondary: bg-white text-slate-700 border border-slate-300 hover:bg-slate-50 rounded-sm px-4 py-2 font-medium transition-colors
buttons.destructive: bg-red-600 text-white hover:bg-red-700 rounded-sm px-4 py-2 font-medium shadow-sm
buttons.ghost: hover:bg-slate-100 text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 rounded-sm px-3 py-2

cards.default: bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-sm shadow-sm overflow-hidden
cards.interactive: bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-sm shadow-sm hover:shadow-md hover:border-slate-300 transition-all cursor-pointer

tables.wrapper: w-full overflow-auto rounded-sm border border-slate-200 bg-white
tables.header_cell: px-4 py-3 text-xs font-semibold text-slate-500 uppercase tracking-wider

inputs.default: flex h-9 w-full rounded-sm border border-slate-300 bg-transparent px-3 py-1 text-sm shadow-sm transition-colors file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-sm file:font-medium placeholder:text-slate-400 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-1 focus-visible:ring-slate-900 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50

images.description: Grayscale industrial machine, high contrast, perfect for split-screen login page background.
Clean metallic wall, useful for headers or empty state backgrounds.
Busy factory floor, use for 'Production Overview' dashboard empty state or welcome screen.

gantt_chart.features: Must support drag-and-drop, zoom levels (Day, Week, Month), and critical path highlighting.

scheduler_engine.visuals: Use a 'console' or 'terminal' like view to show OR-Tools solver logs. Give it a tech/hacker vibe with monospace font and dark background.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. FILE STRUCTURE: Organize components by domain (e.g., components/scheduler, components/inventory) rather than just 'ui'.
2. NO ROUNDED CORNERS: Force 'rounded-sm' (2px) or 'rounded-none' for a strict industrial look. Default Tailwind 'rounded-lg' is forbidden.
3. DENSITY: Override default shadcn padding. Change 'p-4' to 'p-3' or 'p-2' in table cells.
4. DATA VISUALIZATION: For the Gantt chart, ensure it takes up significant screen real estate. It is the core feature.
5. EXPLICABILITY: When showing scheduling results, use a split view: Gantt on top, 'Decision Log' (why this machine? why this time?) on the bottom.
6. INSTALLATION: You must install 'lucide-react', 'recharts' (for stats), and 'gantt-task-react' (or similar).
7. TESTING: Add 'data-testid' to every button, input, and row. Key workflows must be testable.
universal_guidelines: Do not use generic centered layouts.
Create depth through layered design elements (z-index) but keep it flat/matte, not glossy.
Use 'IBM Plex Sans' for body text.
Use 'JetBrains Mono' for all data tables and metrics.
